title: Police declare terrorist incident after two Jewish men stabbed in London
summary_cn: 伦敦北部戈尔德斯格林发生一起持刀袭击事件，两名犹太男子被刺伤，警方已将其定性为恐怖事件。一名45岁索马里裔英国籍嫌疑人被警方电击逮捕，目前被拘留。英国首相斯塔默谴责此次袭击为‘令人发指的反犹太主义行为’，并承诺加强犹太社区安全保护。
paragraphs_en: Two Jewish men have been stabbed in Golders Green, north London, in what has been declared a terrorist incident by police.
The two men, aged 76 and 34, were treated at the scene and are now in a stable condition in hospital, the Metropolitan Police said.
A 45-year-old man, a British national born in Somalia, was Tasered by police before being arrested on suspicion of attempted murder, the force said. The suspect is now in custody.
Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer said the "antisemitic attack" - the latest in a string in the UK - was "utterly appalling", adding: "Attacks on our Jewish community are attacks on Britain."
The Met said its officers responded at 11:16 BST on Wednesday after receiving reports that people had been stabbed in Highfield Avenue, which runs off Golders Green Road.
The suspect also attempted to stab the attending officers, the Met said, but none were injured.
Video shared on social media appears to show the moment of the arrest. Two officers can be seen approaching the man before one Tasers him and he falls to the ground.

The man appears to then hold a sharp object to his chest with both hands, and the officers and a bystander attempt to wrest it from him. The officers can be seen kicking the man in the head several times in the struggle.

The Met released footage of the incident from the officers' body-worn cameras, in which they can be heard shouting repeatedly for the suspect to "get down on the ground" before he is Tasered, and then "drop the knife".

"The suspect refused to show his hands, was violent and continued to pose a clear threat," the force said, adding that the suspect "continued to try to attack and stab" the officers as they were detaining him.
Met Police Commissioner Sir Mark Rowley paid tribute to the officers' courage, saying: "These were not armed officers and they feared he was carrying an explosive device."
The suspect had a history of serious violence and mental health issues, he added in a statement at the scene on Wednesday afternoon.
The Met later said officers were carrying out a search at an address in south-east London. Detectives believe the same suspect was involved in an earlier incident in the area.
Police were called at 08:50 BST on Tuesday to an address on Great Dover Street in Southwark, after the suspect was reportedly involved in an altercation with the property's occupant.
The occupant suffered "minor injuries" and attending officers were unable to locate the suspect, who had left.
Eyewitness Daniela had been shopping in Golders Green on Wednesday morning when she heard people screaming: "He's got a knife, run."
The 29-year-old told the BBC that she took shelter in a nearby bookshop and saw the suspect run past in the direction of the North Circular Road.
"At that point, no one was on the street – everyone had run," she said. "We were a group of mums with buggies. The shop owner locked the door. We were speechless."
CCTV footage appears to show a man lunging at a person standing near a bus stop. In another clip, a man wearing similar clothing can be seen jogging down a street before chasing a passerby.
Sir Keir thanked Shomrim, the Jewish security group whose responders initially detained the suspect, as well as the volunteer-led ambulance service Hatzola and the police, for "acting swiftly".
Shomrim said it dispatched volunteers after receiving a call concerning a man running along Golders Green Road "armed with a knife and attempting to stab Jewish members of the public".
Ben Grossnass, a co-ordinator for the charity, told the BBC that its volunteers "were the first on scene".
The head of Counter Terrorism Policing, Assistant Commissioner Laurence Taylor, later formally declared the attack a terrorist incident.
The Met commissioner said his "first thoughts" were with the victims, who had been attacked "whilst going about their daily lives, which they should be able to do freely and safely".

Sir Mark added: "Whilst I can't comment on live investigations, we know that some individuals are being encouraged, persuaded or paid to commit acts of violence on behalf of foreign organisations and hostile states."
While the Met Police chief spoke, he was heckled by bystanders, who shouted "shame on you" and "you've failed", and called for his resignation. The local MP, Sarah Sackman, was also heckled.
She said she understood the anger of the hecklers and insisted tackling antisemitism was "a fight we need to have society-wide".
Chief Rabbi Sir Ephraim Mirvis, who represents Britain's largest Jewish community, said the incident "proves that if you are visibly Jewish, you're not safe and far more needs to be done".
"This must be a moment that demands meaningful action from every institution, every community, every leader and every decent person in our country," he said.

Wednesday's attack comes less than a week after a suspected arson attack against a memorial wall in Golders Green, and little over a month since ambulances operated by Jewish medical charity Hatzola were set alight .
A synagogue in Kenton, north London, was petrol-bombed earlier this month , while two Jewish people were killed in a stabbing attack on a synagogue in Manchester in October.
London Mayor Sadiq Khan said he was "angry", "appalled" and "disgusted" that Jewish people were "living their lives frightened".
"I've never experienced this level of attacks on Jewish people across our country," he said.
King Charles III was "naturally deeply concerned" by Wednesday's attack, a Buckingham Palace spokesperson said.
"His thoughts and prayers are with the two individuals who were injured and offers his heartfelt gratitude to those who so selflessly rushed to their aid," they added.

Asked whether the government was doing enough to keep British Jews safe, Sir Keir said he wanted to "look at enhanced security" and increase funding that was already in place for protecting Jewish communities.
He added that measures "to deal with malign state actors" were also needed. Several previous antisemitic attacks have been linked to unfriendly regimes .
The prime minister's comments came after he chaired an emergency cabinet meeting concerning the attack with Home Secretary Shabana Mahmood and London Mayor Sadiq Khan, among others.
Earlier, Mahmood said the government would provide "an enhanced police presence that will continue", adding: "We will strain every sinew to keep our Jewish community safe."
Leader of the Conservative Party Kemi Badenoch said there was now "an epidemic of violence against Jewish people" that had become "a national emergency".
Reform UK leader Nigel Farage said the attack was "abhorrent, appalling, but sadly now totally unsurprising", adding: "It is open season on Jewish people in this country."
Liberal Democrats leader Sir Ed Davey said the incident was "horrifying" and called on the government to "confront antisemitism wherever it appears"
Zack Polanski, leader of the Green Party, described the attack as "horrendous" and said he was "thinking of the victims, their families and everyone who will once again be shaken by this attack".
paragraphs_cn: 两名犹太男子在伦敦北部戈尔德斯格林（Golders Green）被刺伤，警方已将此事件宣布为恐怖袭击。
伦敦警察厅表示，两名受害者年龄分别为76岁和34岁，在现场接受了治疗，目前在医院情况稳定。
警方称，一名45岁的男子（出生于索马里的英国公民）被警方用电击枪制服，随后因涉嫌谋杀未遂被捕。该嫌疑人目前被拘留。
首相基尔·斯塔默爵士（Sir Keir Starmer）表示，这起“反犹太主义袭击”——英国近期一系列类似事件中的最新一起——是“极其令人震惊的”，并补充说：“对我们犹太社区的袭击就是对英国的袭击。”
伦敦警察厅表示，其警员于周三11:16（英国夏令时）接到报告称海菲尔德大道（Highfield Avenue，位于戈尔德斯格林路旁）发生刺伤事件后出警。
伦敦警察厅称，嫌疑人还试图刺伤出警警员，但无人受伤。
社交媒体上分享的视频似乎显示了逮捕的瞬间。可以看到两名警员接近该男子，其中一人用电击枪将其击倒。
该男子随后双手将一件尖锐物品抵在胸前，警员和一名旁观者试图夺下该物品。在搏斗中，可以看到警员多次踢该男子的头部。
伦敦警察厅发布了警员随身摄像头拍摄的事件视频，视频中可以听到警员在电击前反复喊嫌疑人“趴在地上”，随后喊“放下刀”。
警方表示：“嫌疑人拒绝露出双手，行为暴力，并继续构成明显威胁。”并补充说，嫌疑人在被拘留过程中“继续试图攻击和刺伤”警员。
伦敦警察厅专员马克·罗利爵士（Sir Mark Rowley）赞扬了警员的勇气，称：“这些警员没有配枪，他们担心嫌疑人携带爆炸装置。”
他周三下午在现场的一份声明中补充说，嫌疑人有严重的暴力史和精神健康问题。
伦敦警察厅后来表示，警员正在伦敦东南部一处地址进行搜查。侦探认为同一嫌疑人涉及该地区早些时候的一起事件。
警方于周二08:50（英国夏令时）接到南华克区大杜弗街（Great Dover Street）一处地址的报警，据报嫌疑人与该房产的住户发生争执。
该住户受了“轻伤”，出警警员未能找到已离开的嫌疑人。
目击者丹妮埃拉（Daniela）周三早上在戈尔德斯格林购物时听到人们尖叫：“他拿着刀，快跑。”
这位29岁的女士告诉BBC，她躲进附近一家书店，看到嫌疑人跑向北环路方向。
她说：“那时街上空无一人——所有人都跑了。我们是一群推着婴儿车的妈妈。店主锁了门。我们都说不出话来。”
监控录像似乎显示一名男子扑向站在公交车站附近的人。在另一段视频中，可以看到一名穿着类似衣服的男子沿街慢跑，然后追赶一名路人。
基尔爵士感谢犹太安全组织Shomrim（其响应者最初拘留了嫌疑人）、志愿者领导的救护服务Hatzola以及警方“迅速采取行动”。
Shomrim表示，在接到关于一名男子“持刀沿戈尔德斯格林路奔跑并试图刺伤犹太公众”的电话后，他们派遣了志愿者。
该慈善机构的协调员本·格罗斯纳斯（Ben Grossnass）告诉BBC，其志愿者“是第一批到达现场的”。
反恐警务负责人、助理专员劳伦斯·泰勒（Laurence Taylor）随后正式宣布此次袭击为恐怖事件。
伦敦警察厅专员表示，他的“首要关切”是受害者，他们在“日常活动中”遭到袭击，“他们本应能够自由安全地生活”。
马克爵士补充说：“虽然我不能对正在进行的调查发表评论，但我们知道，有些人正在被鼓励、说服或收买，代表外国组织和敌对国家实施暴力行为。”
在伦敦警察厅专员讲话时，他被旁观者质问，他们高喊“羞耻”和“你失败了”，并呼吁他辞职。当地议员萨拉·萨克曼（Sarah Sackman）也遭到质问。
她表示理解质问者的愤怒，并坚称打击反犹太主义是“我们需要全社会进行的斗争”。
代表英国最大犹太社区的首席拉比埃弗莱姆·米尔维斯爵士（Sir Ephraim Mirvis）表示，这一事件“证明，如果你明显是犹太人，你就不安全，还需要做更多工作”。
他说：“这必须是一个要求我国每个机构、每个社区、每个领导人和每个正派人士采取有意义行动的时刻。”
周三的袭击发生不到一周前，戈尔德斯格林的一处纪念墙遭到疑似纵火袭击；一个多月前，犹太医疗慈善机构Hatzola运营的救护车被纵火。
本月早些时候，伦敦北部肯顿（Kenton）的一座犹太教堂遭到汽油弹袭击；10月，曼彻斯特一座犹太教堂发生持刀袭击事件，两名犹太人被杀。
伦敦市长萨迪克·汗（Sadiq Khan）表示，他对犹太人“生活在恐惧中”感到“愤怒”、“震惊”和“厌恶”。
他说：“我从未经历过我国针对犹太人的这种程度的袭击。”
白金汉宫发言人表示，国王查尔斯三世（King Charles III）对周三的袭击“自然深感关切”。
他们补充说：“他的思念和祈祷与两名受伤者同在，并对那些无私伸出援手的人表示衷心感谢。”
当被问及政府是否在保护英国犹太人安全方面做得足够时，基尔爵士表示，他希望“考虑加强安保”，并增加已经用于保护犹太社区的资助。
他补充说，还需要“处理恶意国家行为者”的措施。此前几起反犹太主义袭击与不友好政权有关。
首相的评论是在他主持了关于此次袭击的紧急内阁会议后发表的，内政大臣沙巴纳·马哈茂德（Shabana Mahmood）和伦敦市长萨迪克·汗等人出席了会议。
早些时候，马哈茂德表示，政府将提供“持续的加强警力”，并补充说：“我们将竭尽全力保护我们的犹太社区安全。”
保守党领袖凯米·巴德诺赫（Kemi Badenoch）表示，现在存在“针对犹太人的暴力流行病”，已成为“国家紧急状态”。
改革英国党领袖奈杰尔·法拉奇（Nigel Farage）表示，这次袭击“令人憎恶、震惊，但遗憾的是现在完全不令人意外”，并补充说：“在这个国家，对犹太人来说现在是开放季节。”
自由民主党领袖埃德·戴维爵士（Sir Ed Davey）表示，这一事件“令人恐惧”，并呼吁政府“在反犹太主义出现的地方予以对抗”。
绿党领袖扎克·波兰斯基（Zack Polanski）将此次袭击描述为“可怕的”，并表示他“想到受害者、他们的家人以及所有将再次被这次袭击震动的人”。

keywords.definition_en: to push a knife or other sharp object into someone
keywords.context_sentence: Two Jewish men have been stabbed in Golders Green, north London, in what has been declared a terrorist incident by police.
keywords.context_translation: 两名犹太男子在伦敦北部戈尔德斯格林被刺伤，警方已宣布这是一起恐怖事件。

keywords.definition_en: to state something officially or formally
keywords.context_sentence: Two Jewish men have been stabbed in Golders Green, north London, in what has been declared a terrorist incident by police.
keywords.context_translation: 两名犹太男子在伦敦北部戈尔德斯格林被刺伤，警方已宣布这是一起恐怖事件。

keywords.definition_en: a person who is believed to have committed a crime
keywords.context_sentence: A 45-year-old man, a British national born in Somalia, was Tasered by police before being arrested on suspicion of attempted murder, the force said. The suspect is now in custody.
keywords.context_translation: 警方称，一名45岁的男子，出生于索马里的英国公民，在被捕前被警方电击，涉嫌谋杀未遂。该嫌疑犯现已被拘留。

keywords.definition_en: the state of being kept in prison or under police control
keywords.context_sentence: A 45-year-old man, a British national born in Somalia, was Tasered by police before being arrested on suspicion of attempted murder, the force said. The suspect is now in custody.
keywords.context_translation: 警方称，一名45岁的男子，出生于索马里的英国公民，在被捕前被警方电击，涉嫌谋杀未遂。该嫌疑犯现已被拘留。

keywords.definition_cn: 令人震惊的，骇人听闻的
keywords.context_sentence: Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer said the "antisemitic attack" - the latest in a string in the UK - was "utterly appalling", adding: "Attacks on our Jewish community are attacks on Britain."
keywords.context_translation: 首相基尔·斯塔默爵士表示，这次“反犹太主义袭击”——英国一系列袭击中的最新一起——“完全令人震惊”，并补充说：“对我们犹太社区的袭击就是对英国的袭击。”

keywords.definition_en: to keep someone in custody or prevent them from leaving
keywords.context_sentence: The suspect refused to show his hands, was violent and continued to pose a clear threat," the force said, adding that the suspect "continued to try to attack and stab" the officers as they were detaining him.
keywords.context_translation: 警方表示：“嫌疑犯拒绝出示双手，暴力且持续构成明显威胁，”并补充说，在警方拘留他时，他“继续试图攻击和刺伤”警察。

keywords.context_sentence: Police were called at 08:50 BST on Tuesday to an address on Great Dover Street in Southwark, after the suspect was reportedly involved in an altercation with the property's occupant.
keywords.context_translation: 周二英国夏令时08:50，警方接到报警前往萨瑟克区大杜佛街的一处地址，此前据报嫌疑犯与该房产的住户发生了争执。

keywords.definition_en: to make a sudden forward movement, especially to attack someone
keywords.context_sentence: CCTV footage appears to show a man lunging at a person standing near a bus stop.
keywords.context_translation: 闭路电视画面似乎显示一名男子扑向站在公交车站附近的人。
